top_weaknesses.issue: Headline promises Zendesk but lede leads with ServiceNow + macro stats; first 155 chars never says 'Zendesk', wasting the snippet and weakening relevance match
top_weaknesses.fix: Rewrite lede so 'Zendesk' appears in sentence 1 — e.g., 'Zendesk shows the AI displacement signature: ARR slows, seats shrink, LBO math breaks, BDC marks haircut'

top_weaknesses.issue: 'Four-step pattern' is written as First/Second/Third/Fourth prose but never formatted as a list — easy scan win wasted; same for the daisy chain (literally a dependency chain)
top_weaknesses.fix: Convert First/Second/Third/Fourth into a numbered list (or H3s); render the daisy chain as an arrow list (SaaS LBO → BDC → life insurer → annuity → mortgage)

top_weaknesses.issue: Narrative certainty outruns evidence — 'cleanest worked example', 'moat-collapse signature', specific Sept 2027 default + 58c marks asserted as if real
top_weaknesses.fix: Frame Citrini's numbers as 'in the scenario'/'projection'; cut 'cleanest worked example' and 'signature' framing; let the four steps carry the argument

top_weaknesses.issue: H2s slightly abstract for the punchy title — 'The four-step pattern' / 'The daisy chain pulls everything else down' don't preview content
top_weaknesses.fix: Sharpen to 'The 4-step AI moat-collapse pattern' / 'How losses propagate: private credit → insurers → households'

top_weaknesses.issue: Em-dash overuse and essay-scaffolding phrasing ('The pattern moves in a predictable order', 'The dangerous part is what sits behind...', 'This is the structural problem')
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut three of the em-dash parentheticals; replace 'The dangerous part is what sits behind' with the chain itself

consensus_summary: Strong conceptual spine and one quotable line ('A recession lifts when demand recovers. A moat collapse does not lift') but on-page packaging undersells it: lede buries Zendesk, the four-step pattern isn't a list, and the certainty outruns the citations. Ship after a lede rewrite + list conversion + caveat pass.
